otherChedTypesHaveMultipleRadioGroups.observation: On CHED-A and CHED-P this page carries MORE THAN ONE radio group: the workflows check 'Internal market' AND THEN a second radio in the same page visit without any intervening submit. So the shared template has a purpose group plus a second sub-purpose group for those types. CHED-PP has exactly ONE group (name="purpose", 3 radios) — confirmed by the trace control inventory, which returns 3 radios all with name="purpose" and nothing else.
otherChedTypesHaveMultipleRadioGroups.whyItMatters: This is the mechanical reason the page object's radioInternalMarket getter needs .first() (AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:10-12) — 'Internal market' is not unique on the shared page for every CHED type. The CHED-PP rebuild needs neither the second group nor the .first() disambiguation.
otherChedTypesHaveMultipleRadioGroups.firstDisambiguationIsSelective: RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17 by independent grep of the whole page object. The .first() appears on EXACTLY TWO getters — radioInternalMarket (:11) and radioNonInternalMarket (:23) — and on NO other radio getter (radioReEntry :7, radioHumanConsumption :15, radioAnimalFeedingstuff :19, radioTransferOfOwnershipRescue :27, radioTransit :31, radioTemporaryAdmissionHorses :35 are all bare getByRole). That selectivity is itself evidence: an author only reaches for .first() after a strict-mode violation, so 'Internal market' and 'Non-internal market' are the only two accessible names that genuinely collide on the shared page — and they plausibly collide with EACH OTHER, since getByRole name matching is substring-based by default and 'Non-internal market' CONTAINS 'internal market'. If so the .first() on radioInternalMarket may be masking a locator bug rather than a second radio group. This does not affect the CHED-PP rebuild (only 3 radios, no collision) but it weakens this entry's inference slightly — see openQuestions.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: <fieldset class="govuk-fieldset"> with no <legend>; the H1 sits outside the fieldset
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Groups the radios visually/semantically but gives the group no accessible name. The a11y tree shows an unnamed `group [ref=e63]`.
nonStandardPatterns.concern:
Breaks the GOV.UK 'one question per page' radios pattern, which requires the H1 to BE the legend (

...

). As built, a screen reader entering the radio group announces no question — a WCAG 3.3.2 / 1.3.1 concern. The rebuild must use the standard legend-wraps-H1 markup. NOTE: the QA repo's axe run does NOT flag this (axe has no rule for an empty fieldset legend), so its absence from the a11y log is not a clean bill of health — the trace evidence stands.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Radios with fieldset legend as page heading (govuk-fieldset__legend--xl + govuk-fieldset__heading)
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 29 (main.outerHTML — the fieldset opens and the very next element is the radios div, with only whitespace between; no <legend> anywhere in main. a11y snapshot shows unnamed `group [ref=e63]` directly containing the radio items). INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17 by this adversarial pass from both the raw outerHTML and the a11y tree. Confirmed.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Duplicate DOM id: id="re-entry-hint" used on BOTH hint divs
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The 'Internal market' hint and the 'Re-entry' hint both carry id="re-entry-hint".
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Invalid HTML (ids must be unique) and clearly a copy-paste defect — the Internal market hint is misnamed after Re-entry. Combined with the missing aria-describedby (below) the ids are dead weight. The rebuild should use per-radio ids (e.g. purpose-internal-market-item-hint) as govuk-frontend's Nunjucks macro generates automatically. NOTE: the QA repo's axe run does not flag this either — axe's duplicate-id rule was deprecated/removed in axe 4.x, and the run is restricted to wcag2a/2aa/21a/21aa tags (accessibility-utils.ts:119). Absence from the a11y log is not evidence of absence.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-frontend radios macro auto-generates unique hint ids and wires aria-describedby
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 29 (main.outerHTML — id="re-entry-hint" appears on both the Internal market hint div and the Re-entry hint div). RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17 from raw outerHTML: confirmed, both hint divs carry the identical id. SOURCE CONFIRMATION ALSO RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17 BY INDEPENDENT GREP — the defect is in the shared template itself, where the SAME id is hardcoded FOUR times: /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/views/importer/consignmentPurpose.html:73, :222, :233, :304 (grep for 're-entry-hint' returns exactly these four lines). So it is not a rendering accident of the CHED-PP path but a template-wide copy-paste defect affecting every CHED type. Line :304 compounds it, exactly as previously recorded and now re-confirmed verbatim: `<div class="govuk-hint govuk-radios__hint" for="a_impadm2" id="re-entry-hint">` — a `for` attribute on a <div>, which is meaningless in HTML (for belongs on <label>/<output>) and looks like a failed attempt at exactly the input-hint association that is missing throughout.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Hints not associated with their radios — no aria-describedby on any input
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The two hint divs have ids but no radio input references them via aria-describedby.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: The hint text is not programmatically associated with the control, so screen reader users do not hear the explanation of what 'Internal market' or 'Re-entry' means — and that explanation is the load-bearing content of this page. Accessibility defect to fix, not port.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk-frontend radios macro sets aria-describedby on each input automatically
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 29 (control-inventory eval returned desc=null for all five inputs in main; no aria-describedby attribute present anywhere in main.outerHTML). INDEPENDENTLY RE-RUN 2026-07-17 by this adversarial pass — the eval returns desc=null for every input on the whole document (all 8, including the header/footer ones), and a read of the raw main.outerHTML confirms the string 'aria-describedby' does not occur. Confirmed.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: govuk-radios--conditional modifier with no conditional reveal content
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The radios container declares the conditional-reveal modifier, but no govuk-radios__conditional child exists and no input carries data-aria-controls or aria-controls.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Dead/vestigial markup — the modifier does nothing here. Almost certainly bleed-through from the shared all-CHED-types template (see sharedPageWarning): CHED-A/P/D DO have reveals behind these radios, CHED-PP does not. Do not carry it into the rebuild; use plain govuk-radios unless a reveal is genuinely needed. This is the ONLY surviving trace of the conditional-reveal machinery on the current CHED-PP page — see the historical pattern below for the April-2026 state, which was worse.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 29 (main.outerHTML — `class="govuk-radios govuk-radios--conditional" data-module="govuk-radios" id="purpose" data-govuk-radios-init=""` with no govuk-radios__conditional child anywhere; control-inventory eval returns ac=null and dac=null for every input). INDEPENDENTLY RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17 on both counts. TRIPLE-CORROBORATED: trace DOM (no reveal), QA page object (no CHED-PP reveal getters), server schema (getChedppSchema spreads no conditional rule blocks) — and now a FOURTH source, the observed POST body, which carries only crumb/etag/purpose/button-save-and-continue (see observedPostBody). A reveal that renders nothing, accepts no POST data and has no server rule is inert beyond reasonable doubt.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: HISTORICAL (April 2026, NOT present in the July trace corpus): dangling conditional-reveal wiring — #radio-internalmarket carried aria-controls="internalmarket-conditional" pointing at a non-existent element
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: In an axe run captured on 1 April 2026, the CHED-PP 'Internal market' radio referenced a conditional-reveal container id 'internalmarket-conditional' that was not present in the DOM, producing a CRITICAL 'aria-valid-attr-value' violation. The July 2026 trace corpus shows NO such attribute — the wiring is gone; only the inert govuk-radios--conditional class remains.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: NOT A CURRENT DEFECT — do not report it to stakeholders as one. Two independent facts establish this: (1) DATE GAP — the a11y-test.log artefact has an mtime of 1 April 2026, while the trace corpus was recorded 2026-07-16, i.e. the log is ~3.5 months STALE; (2) POST-INIT PROOF — the July trace DOM carries data-govuk-radios-init="" on the radios container, meaning govuk-frontend's radios JS HAD ALREADY RUN when the snapshot was taken, and after init there is still no aria-controls AND no data-aria-controls on any input. So the attribute is genuinely absent from the current page, not merely un-rewritten. The rebuild guidance is unchanged (carry no conditional wiring).
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Plain Radios with no conditional reveal — no aria-controls, no data-aria-controls
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: ~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/a11y-test.log:9592-9599 — '⚠️  About The Consignment Page [/0/dev/protected/notifications/DRAFT.GB.2026.1425001/consignment/page-5]: 1 accessibility violation(s) … aria-valid-attr-value (critical impact) … Node 1: #radio-internalmarket … Invalid ARIA attribute value: aria-controls="internalmarket-conditional"'. Attribution to CHED-PP is solid: the block is immediately preceded by the ched-pp-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:17:7 worker line (a11y-test.log:9591) and draft DRAFT.GB.2026.1425001 is the CHED-PP run's draft (same id at a11y-test.log:9583, 'Commodity Basic Description Page (first)', also under the ched-pp spec line) — the log interleaves parallel workers, so this draft-id correlation is what makes the attribution sound. Staleness: `ls -l a11y-test.log` → mtime '1 Apr 15:26' vs trace start time 2026-07-16T16:14:02.703Z. Post-init proof: trace e10b7cd5 action 29 main.outerHTML contains data-govuk-radios-init="" on div#purpose — RE-VERIFIED VERBATIM 2026-07-17 from raw outerHTML by this adversarial pass; the attribute is present exactly as claimed, so the post-init argument holds.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Custom class "purpose" on each govuk-radios__item; id="purpose" on the radios container
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The only non-govuk-* class in main. Presumably an IPAFFS-specific styling/JS hook.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Low-risk but non-standard. The container id duplicates the input name attribute, which is a smell. No govuk equivalent needed — the rebuild should simply drop it.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: None needed — remove; govuk-radios__item alone is sufficient
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 29 (main.outerHTML — class="govuk-radios__item purpose" on all three items; 'purpose' is the sole non-govuk- class in main). RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17 from raw outerHTML: confirmed on all three items, and confirmed as the only non-govuk- class present.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Each govuk-radios__label also carries the bold font-weight override utility.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not standard GOV.UK styling — Design System radio labels are regular weight. This is a deliberate visual deviation (likely to make the label stand out from its hint). It stays inside the govuk utility toolbox so it is not a hard problem, but the rebuild should only keep it if design confirms it is intended.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Default Radios label styling (no weight override)
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 29 (main.outerHTML — class="govuk-label govuk-radios__label govuk-!-font-weight-bold" on all three labels). RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17: confirmed on all three, including the third (hintless) label.

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: Radio id/value mismatch: id="radio-reimport" carries value="import" for the label 'Re-entry'
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: FOUR different vocabularies for the same concept — form label 'Re-entry', id 'radio-reimport', submitted value 'import', and the view-details display string 'For re-entry'.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Not a markup standards issue but a data-contract trap. Anyone rebuilding from the labels alone would guess a value of 're-entry' or 'reentry' and break the contract with downstream/backend. The submitted values are the load-bearing part: internalmarket | import | reconformity. Pin these in the new app's tests if the wire contract must be preserved; otherwise take the chance to make them consistent. The QA suite independently pins the DISPLAY strings on the view-details page ('For internal market', 'For re-entry'), which is a fifth vocabulary layer to preserve or deliberately simplify. NOTE the extra sting: the CHED-A schema DOES accept a literal 're-entry' value (consignment_purpose_validation.js:211) — so 're-entry' is a real value in this system, just not for CHED-PP, which uses 'import' for the same-labelled option. A rebuilder copying across CHED types would silently get this wrong.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: n/a — naming/data-contract issue, not a component issue
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace e10b7cd5117799d60c1e0fbeddc3322eefde1572 action 29 (control inventory eval: id=radio-reimport value=import) — RE-RUN AND CONFIRMED 2026-07-17. Display strings: ~/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-qa-automation/tests/notification/ched-pp/ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:27 and :93 — re-verified a third time 2026-07-17 by reading :72-95 directly: :93 = `await expect(pages.viewDetails.locatorPurposeOfConsignment).toContainText(/^\s*For re-entry\s*$/);` — exact. The display layer has a DOM contract too: locatorPurposeOfConsignment resolves to `#purpose-of-consignment-value` (page-objects/notification/ViewDetailsPage.ts:29-31). So the full chain for one concept is SIX artefacts: form label 'Re-entry' → input id 'radio-reimport' → wire value 'import' → amend change-link id '#consignment-purpose-change-link' → view-details element id '#purpose-of-consignment-value' → display string 'For re-entry'. Both anchored regexes (^...$) mean the QA suite pins the display strings EXACTLY, so they are a hard contract for anything that reuses these tests. Cross-type 're-entry' value re-verified 2026-07-17: consignment_purpose_validation.js:211 = `...PURPOSE_VALIDATION_RULES(['internalmarket', 'import', 'tranship', 'transit', 're-entry', 'temporary'])`.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The 'Back' link has no real target; it relies on JS history.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Non-progressive-enhancement — fails without JS. The GOV.UK Back link component expects a real URL. Minor, and it lives in the service header chrome rather than the page, but worth fixing in the rebuild.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Back link (govuk-back-link) pointing at the previous page's real URL

fields.name: purpose
fields.label: What is the main reason for importing the consignment?
fields.requiredNote: purpose is MANDATORY, enforced server-side by Joi — `.empty('').required()` in getChedppSchema (consignment_purpose_validation.js:247-253). This field records the REQUIREMENT, not the DOM attribute: no input carries a `required` attribute, which must NOT be read as 'optional'. Mechanism note for the rebuild: IPAFFS does no client-side required validation on this page at all — it round-trips to the server and re-renders with an error. See validationMessages and serverSideContract. RE-VERIFIED 2026-07-17 by reading the schema directly (see serverSideContract evidence).
fields.requiredNoteLegacy: MANDATORINESS NOW CORROBORATED FROM THE AUTHORITATIVE BACKEND SCHEMA MODEL (legacy source, authorised 2026-07-17), not merely the frontend Joi layer. The rebuild-target model is still built like trade-imports-animals and NOT like IPAFFS, but IPAFFS's own Bean Validation groups are the definitive record of what the old system required for CHED-PP: (1) PartOne.purpose carries @NotNull for the NotificationChedppFieldValidation group — /Users/samfarrington/git/defra/ipaffs/ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/notification-schema-java/src/main/java/uk/gov/defra/tracesx/notificationschema/representation/PartOne.java:347-358 (the `groups = { ... NotificationChedppFieldValidation.class ... }` block on `private Purpose purpose;` at :372), message key `partone.purpose.not.null`; (2) the actual radio selection is the model field Purpose.purposeGroup (a PurposeGroupEnum), ALSO @NotNull for NotificationChedppFieldValidation — Purpose.java:66-78, message key `partone.purpose.purposeGroup.not.null`. So both the purpose object AND its purposeGroup discriminator are mandatory for CHED-PP at the schema layer. This is a policy the rebuild MAY revisit, but for a core discriminator field the risk of revisiting is low. confidence: legacy.
fields.radioToModelMapping: LEGACY (template-confirmed, 2026-07-17). The three CHED-PP radios bind their checked-state to the model field purposeGroup (PurposeGroupEnum), giving a fifth vocabulary layer on top of the four already recorded in nonStandardPatterns. Mapping (wire value → PurposeGroupEnum constant → enum display value used for the checked test in the template): `internalmarket` → IMPORT → 'For Import' (consignmentPurpose.html:69); `import` (label 'Re-entry') → RE_IMPORT → 'For Re-Import' (consignmentPurpose.html:218/229); `reconformity` → RE_CONFORMITY_CHECK → 'For Import Re-Conformity Check' (consignmentPurpose.html:372). serverSideContract.summary: Derived from the IPAFFS frontend source (see the provenance flag in openQuestions — source is NOT one of this exercise's two admissible evidence sources). The CHED-PP POST schema is small, explicit, and independently confirms three things the trace evidence could only suggest. AS OF 2026-07-17 several of its claims are no longer source-only: the observed POST body (see observedPostBody) corroborates them from rendered reality.
confirms: purpose IS MANDATORY — .required() server-side. The absence of a DOM `required` attribute is not evidence of optionality. Still source-only: no trace or test ever submitted an empty purpose.

The CHED-PP purpose set is EXACTLY the 3 observed values and no more — .valid('internalmarket','import','reconformity') matches the 3 rendered radios one-for-one. The radio set is NOT conditional on commodity/origin/anything upstream; it is a fixed literal in the schema. Corroborated by trace: the set is identical across a manual flow, a CSV flow AND an EU CHED-PP flow.

CHED-PP has NO conditional-reveal fields behind purpose. getChedppSchema spreads ONLY the purpose rule plus etag and save-actions — it does NOT spread INTERNAL_MARKET_VALIDATION_RULES, TRANSIT_*, TRANSHIPMENT_*, TEMPORARY_ADMISSION_*, CUSTOMS_*, FREE_ZONE_*, SHIP_* or any of the other conditional rule blocks that the CHED-A (:198-220) and CHED-P (:222-245) schemas do. NOW CORROBORATED BY TRACE: the observed POST body carries exactly 4 fields (observedPostBody), so no reveal data is transmitted in practice either.

The etag hidden field is a genuine part of the contract (ETAG_SCHEMA is spread into every CHED schema), not incidental markup. NOW CONFIRMED BY TRACE — the observed POST body contains etag=%220000000000BD2C0B%22.

The named submit button is part of the contract too (SAVE_ACTIONS_SCHEMA) — the server branches on which save action was posted, which is consistent with the inferred 'Save and review' amend variant in secondaryActions. adversarialReview.verdict: corrected — the spec was overwhelmingly sound; corrections are upgrades, not retractions
adversarialReview.whatWasChecked: Every `confirmed` copy string was re-derived from the raw trace DOM and diffed character-for-character (caption, H1, all three labels, both hints, button text, page title) — ALL EXACT, no invented or tidied copy. The evidence pointer was verified to land on the right page (action 29 is a Check on the Internal market radio, page title 'Purpose of the consignment', H1 matches). The control inventory was re-run independently and diffed against the field list — no missed controls in main. All three evidence traces were re-opened and confirmed to render byte-identical main innerText. Every source citation (IPAFFS templates, Joi validation, en.js message/label catalogues, QA page objects, workflows and specs) was re-checked by fresh grep/read — all exact, including the four-times-duplicated re-entry-hint id and the `for="a_impadm2"` oddity on a <div>.
findings: NO invented copy found. NO fabricated options found. NO confidence inflation found. NO wrong-page error. The spec's `confirmed` claims are all genuinely observed and its `inferred` claims are all genuinely source-only and honestly labelled — including the ones it flags against itself.

The only defects found were UNDER-claiming and one uncited claim: (1) `url` was the sole claim in the whole spec with no evidence — now confirmed from trace network evidence; (2) the hub-landing behaviour was rated `inferred` from QA-workflow shape when the trace's own POST 302 Location header confirms it outright — upgraded to `confirmed`, and the hub's real URL /overview recorded for the first time; (3) the actual POST body had never been recovered from the trace, so several serverSideContract claims sat at `inferred` when trace evidence was available to corroborate them — added as observedPostBody; (4) POST/Redirect/GET was unrecorded; (5) the three option pseudo-fields carry `required: false`, a category artefact that could mislead a rebuilder into reading options as optional inputs — annotated, not restructured.

One cosmetic provenance smell, not corrected: several entries were dated '2026-07-18', which postdates both the trace corpus (2026-07-16) and this review (2026-07-17). The content those entries assert verifies exactly, so this is a clock/labelling artefact rather than a substantive problem, but future-dated verification claims should not be trusted on their face.
adversarialReview.networkEvidenceIsUnderused: GENERAL NOTE FOR OTHER PAGE SPECS IN THIS EXERCISE. This spec — like, presumably, its siblings — was built almost entirely from DOM snapshots, and treated the request/response layer as out of reach. It is not. `trace requests` / `trace request <id>` expose the real GET, the real POST body (via the trace's own resource store) and the real response headers, and they resolved in one pass three things that the DOM alone had left as `inferred` or uncited: the page's absolute URL, the redirect target, and the exact wire payload. Any sibling spec whose open questions include 'what does this page actually submit?' or 'where does Save and continue go?' can likely close them the same way, WITHOUT resorting to the legacy source that the exercise's evidence rules exclude. Recommend a network-evidence sweep across the other CHED-PP page specs.

openQuestions: PROVENANCE FLAG — LARGELY RESOLVED 2026-07-17. The IPAFFS application source (Handlebars templates, route handlers, Joi validation, en.js catalogues, AND the backend schema model + ValidationMessages.properties) is NOW AN AUTHORISED `legacy` evidence source for COPY, FIELD VALUES, VALIDATION RULES and MANDATORINESS. Accordingly, the previously-quarantined source-derived findings have been promoted from `inferred` to `legacy` where they are verbatim reads (both validationMessages entries, and the new backendModelContract / requiredNoteLegacy / radioToModelMapping additions). What legacy does NOT settle: PRESENTATION of the error state (never rendered — still an evidence gap; see ERROR-STATE PRESENTATION below), and whether the old system's mandatoriness is a policy the REBUILD should keep — legacy tells you what IPAFFS required, not what the new service must require. The two known copy DEFECTS remain findings for content design, not things to port: 'What is the purpose of the consignment? is not valid' interpolates a question-mark into a sentence, and the required-message 'Select the purpose of the consignment' does not echo the H1 (IPAFFS itself owns better H1-echoing copy for the singleced/CHED-D variants — see validationMessages). ORIGINAL FLAG PRESERVED FOR HISTORY: This exercise's admissible sources are exactly TWO: the rendered frontend (traces) and the QA automation repo. The IPAFFS application source (Handlebars templates, route handlers, Joi validation, en.js message catalogues) was explicitly OUT OF SCOPE, on the stated grounds that inferring requirements from the legacy implementation is the thing this exercise is trying to avoid, and that an honest `gap` is the correct answer where the two admissible sources are silent. A previous revision nevertheless consulted that source and used it to author the entire `serverSideContract` block, BOTH entries in `validationMessages`, the `requiredNote` on the purpose field, and parts of the sharedPageWarning evidence. Those findings are PRESERVED here, not deleted — they are internally consistent, carefully cited to file:line, honestly marked `inferred`, they have now been independently re-verified line-by-line by the 2026-07-17 adversarial pass and found exact, and they are the only validation copy anyone has. But a human should know their provenance before they harden into requirements: they are legacy-implementation-derived, they were gathered outside this exercise's evidence rules, and NOTHING in the traces or the QA repo corroborates them. Under the wave's own rules the correct status of 'what error message does this page show?' is `gap`. Recommend either (a) a human explicitly blesses the source-derived copy as admissible, or (b) it is re-marked `gap` and the copy is written fresh by content design — which the entries themselves recommend anyway, since both messages are demonstrably defective ('What is the purpose of the consignment? is not valid' interpolates a question-mark into a sentence, and the required-message does not echo the H1).

'For import re-conformity check' (value=reconformity) is exercised by NEITHER source — no trace selects it and the shared page object has no getter for it at all (independently re-confirmed 2026-07-17). By contrast 'Re-entry' (value=import) IS proven end-to-end for CHED-PP by the QA suite on the AMEND route: ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:74 selects it, the notification re-submits successfully, and view-details renders 'For re-entry' (:93) — and it did not branch the journey in that test.

NEW 2026-07-17 — is the `.first()` on the page object's radioInternalMarket getter (AboutTheConsignmentPage.ts:11) masking a LOCATOR BUG rather than disambiguating a second radio group? Playwright's getByRole name matching is substring-based and case-insensitive by default, so getByRole('radio', { name: 'Internal market' }) will ALSO match a radio whose accessible name is 'Non-internal market'. Re-verified by independent grep 2026-07-17: the two getters carrying .first() are EXACTLY radioInternalMarket (:11) and radioNonInternalMarket (:23) — no other radio getter in the file has it. NO IMPACT ON THE CHED-PP REBUILD (3 radios, no name collision, no second group — confirmed by trace control inventory). Flagged because it may be a live defect worth reporting to the QA team, and because it slightly weakens a claim elsewhere in this spec.

STALE-ARTEFACT QUESTION. The April a11y log records a CRITICAL dangling aria-controls on #radio-internalmarket; the July trace corpus shows no such attribute. A previous revision reconciled these by claiming govuk-frontend's radios JS rewrites data-aria-controls to aria-controls at init, so a post-JS snapshot would show only the rewritten form. That explanation FAILS on two counts and has been withdrawn: (a) it predicts the trace would show aria-controls, but the trace shows NEITHER aria-controls NOR data-aria-controls (independently re-confirmed 2026-07-17 — the control inventory returns ac=null and dac=null for every input, and 'aria-controls' does not appear anywhere in main.outerHTML); (b) govuk-frontend's radios JS only performs that rewrite when the reveal target EXISTS — if the target is missing it leaves data-aria-controls untouched and never sets aria-controls, so the JS cannot manufacture a dangling aria-controls. The simpler and better-supported reading is that the two artefacts are ~3.5 months apart (log mtime 1 Apr 2026 vs traces 16 Jul 2026) and the markup CHANGED in between — i.e. the dangling wiring was removed, leaving only the inert govuk-radios--conditional class. Worth someone confirming against IPAFFS source/changelog, because if true the April log should not be cited as current evidence of anything. Related and worth noting: the QA repo's axe runner disables the 'aria-allowed-attr' rule outright with the comment 'GOV.UK Frontend v6 adds aria-expanded to conditional radio inputs at runtime — third-party behaviour, not fixable in application code (IMTA-20937)' (accessibility-utils.ts:118-119) — so govuk-frontend v6 DOES mutate conditional radios at runtime, which is presumably what inspired the withdrawn reconciliation, but the mutation it makes is aria-expanded, not aria-controls.

The hidden `etag` field round-trips an optimistic-concurrency token on POST — NOW DIRECTLY OBSERVED on the wire (etag=%220000000000BD2C0B%22, trace 9a918b86 request 1021), not merely implied by the hidden field's presence. Does the rebuild need equivalent lost-update protection on the draft notification, or is that an artefact of IPAFFS's architecture that the new CDP app should drop? What the system DOES on a stale etag is still completely unobserved — no test in the QA repo asserts anything about it, and no trace ever submitted a stale one. Product/architecture decision, not a traceable one.

The submitted value for 'Re-entry' is `import`, not `re-entry` — and its id is `radio-reimport`, and the view-details page displays it as 'For re-entry' (pinned by ched-pp-manipulation.spec.ts:93). Sharpened 2026-07-17: `re-entry` IS a valid value elsewhere in this system — the CHED-A schema accepts it literally (consignment_purpose_validation.js:211) — so the CHED-PP choice of `import` for the same-labelled option is an active trap for anyone generalising across CHED types. Is the wire value `internalmarket|import|reconformity` a contract the new app must preserve (backend/downstream consumers), or is the rebuild free to use clean values? Same question for the display strings 'For internal market' / 'For re-entry'. NEW: the submit button's VALUE is also on the wire (button-save-and-continue=Save+and+continue), so button copy is coupled to the payload too — another contract to break deliberately rather than accidentally.

'For import re-conformity check' is the only option with no hint text, while the other two have substantial explanatory hints. Is that a deliberate content decision or a content gap in IPAFFS? Worth a content-designer view for the rebuild — the term is not self-explanatory. (Note it is also the only option no test drives, which may itself hint that it is little-used or semi-dead surface.)
